Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple The threat actors exploited common vulnerabilities to gain initial access, execute their payload, and exfiltrate sensitive data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Authentication issues

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-11138 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office Memory Corruption Vulnerability
stage.description: The attackers crafted a spear-phishing email targeting healthcare employees, exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office to deliver a malicious attachment.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2021-31201 [CWE-94]: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-Site Scripting')
stage.description: Upon successful execution of the phishing email, the attackers exploited a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability to upload and execute a malicious file on the target web server.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2019-1130 [CWE-78]: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
stage.description: The attackers then exploited an OS command injection vulnerability to execute commands on the compromised system, allowing them to escalate privileges and move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Finally, the attackers exfiltrated sensitive data using removable media, taking advantage of poor physical security controls within the healthcare facility.

Scenario.summary: This simple attack scenario demonstrates how basic threat actors can exploit common vulnerabilities to gain unauthorized access to healthcare systems and exfiltrate sensitive patient data.
